Title: Genetic architecture controlling variation in grain carotenoid composition and concentrations in two maize populations

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DEexp × CI7 F2:3; A619 × SC55 F2:3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes lycopene at the epsilon ring to direct flux into the α-carotene/lutein branch.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-carotene; lutein; α/β branch ratio; total carotenoid profile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provitamin A activity for α-carotene; lutein contributes to nutritional carotenoid quality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation at lcyε is associated with increased α-branch carotenoids, especially α-carotene and lutein, and shifts carotenoid composition toward the α-branch.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifting flux toward the α-branch can reduce allocation to β-branch provitamin A carotenoids such as β-carotene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL colocalization with allele-specific marker mapping in segregating maize populations; carotenoid HPLC profiling; comparison with prior association studies cited by authors.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lcyε mapped to chromosome 8 and coincided with QTL for α-carotene, lutein, and α/β branch ratio in A619 × SC55. 2. The locus exhibited pleiotropic effects on α-branch carotenoids and major variation in lutein-related PC traits. 3. The study supports lcyε as a major determinant of carotenoid branch allocation in maize grain.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats lcyε as a strong candidate underlying QTL rather than proving causality de novo in this experiment; support is strengthened by prior association studies.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown in Illinois and Mexico; genotype-by-environment effects significant for many carotenoid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Already useful for marker-assisted selection to modify carotenoid composition; recommended for breeding programs targeting carotenoid profile and branch allocation, but should be balanced with provitamin A goals.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DEexp × CI7 F2:3; A619 × SC55 F2:3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene to β-cryptoxanthin and further to zeaxanthin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-carotene; β-cryptoxanthin; zeaxanthin; provitamin A; β-carotene isomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-carotene is provitamin A; zeaxanthin and β-cryptoxanthin contribute nutritional carotenoid quality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: crtRB1 alters β-branch conversion, decreasing β-carotene and increasing β-cryptoxanthin/zeaxanthin when hydroxylation is favored; allelic variation strongly modifies provitamin A composition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greater hydroxylation toward β-cryptoxanthin and zeaxanthin can reduce β-carotene accumulation, affecting provitamin A breeding targets depending on allele choice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allele-specific marker mapping in segregating populations; QTL colocalization; HPLC carotenoid profiling; comparison to prior functional/association evidence from Yan et al. 2010.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtRB1 mapped to chromosome 10 and colocalized with QTL for β-carotene, β-cryptoxanthin, zeaxanthin, β-carotene:β-cryptoxanthin ratio, and β-carotene isomer traits. 2. The locus showed pleiotropic effects on β-branch carotenoids in both populations. 3. The study supports crtRB1 as a major determinant of β-branch composition and provitamin A outcome in maize grain.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes prior evidence for crtRB1 effects in DEexp × CI7 and regards this locus as already validated in maize association studies.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown in Illinois and Mexico; genotype-by-environment effects significant for several carotenoid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A major marker-assisted selection target already used to increase β-carotene/provitamin A; useful for compositional modification of β-branch carotenoids and isomer profiles.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DEexp × CI7 F2:3; A619 × SC55 F2:3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves carotenoids, removing them from the accumulation pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: total carotenoids; lutein; zeinoxanthin; β-cryptoxanthin; zeaxanthin; phytoene; phytofluene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carotenoids contribute nutritional quality and provitamin A value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ccd1 is associated with reduction of multiple grain carotenoids through degradation; alleles near this locus explain major decreases in total and individual carotenoid levels.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing CCD1 activity may improve carotenoid retention but could alter apocarotenoid formation; specific tradeoffs were not quantified in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allele-specific marker mapping; QTL colocalization with multiple carotenoid traits; HPLC carotenoid profiling; support from prior association analyses mentioned by authors.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccd1 mapped to chromosome 9 and was proximal to major pleiotropic QTL affecting many carotenoids in A619 × SC55. 2. The SC55 allele near this region was associated with reduced carotenoid levels. 3. The study implicates CCD1-mediated degradation as a major source of variation in total carotenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The mapped ccd1 marker lay between nearby QTL clusters; authors infer likely involvement based on metabolic logic, prior evidence, and pleiotropy rather than direct enzyme assays in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown in Mexico and Illinois depending on population; environment influenced many carotenoid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong candidate for reducing carotenoid loss in breeding; lowering deleterious alleles near CCD1 may enhance total carotenoid accumulation.
